label: vertex
headline_statement: Yes, with the no-kink convention applied to reduced value branches rather than to duplicate LP bases. For $\bar\lambda=\min\{\lambda,1\}$, the leak endpoint pair is computed by the finite response-type LP \eqref{eq:vlp}; its feasible-basis values are rational functions $v_B^U(P,\lambda)=c_B(P,\lambda)^\mathsf{T}A_B(\lambda)^{-1}b(P)$ and $v_B^L(P,\lambda)$, with $U(\lambda;P)=\max_B v_B^U(P,\lambda)$ and $L(\lambda;P)=\min_B v_B^L(P,\lambda)$. Quotient feasible bases whose value functions agree identically on each connected common-feasibility cell, order the remaining branches lexicographically, and define the maximizing branch at each $\lambda$ to be the first branch attaining the envelope. Then the first maximizing-cell threshold is the smallest positive point at which this canonical maximizing branch differs from the canonical branch active at $0^+$; it is found from the finite list of nonzero cleared-denominator branch equalities and feasibility-boundary equalities, and is $+\infty$ if that list produces no positive canonical switch. The minimizing threshold is defined analogously. Every feasible LP value is realized by a shared-$U$ completion with at most two latent atoms, obtained from the arm averages $\bar a^t=p_t^{-1}\sum_a\rho_{t,a}a$, so sharpness respects the locked support restriction $|\mathcal U|\le5$.
